steps.targetDesc: The horizontal bar of icons at the bottom of the screen
steps.why: The Dock is the fastest way to launch Safari without searching
steps.pitfall: If the Dock is hidden, move your mouse to the very bottom edge of the screen to reveal it

steps.targetDesc: A blue compass-rose icon labeled "Safari" in the Dock
steps.why: Identifying the correct icon prevents accidentally opening a different browser
steps.pitfall: Safari is usually near the left side of the Dock; if missing, proceed to Step 3

steps.targetDesc: Single left-click on the blue compass-rose Dock icon
steps.expectedState: Safari bounces in the Dock and its window opens on screen
steps.why: A single click is all that's needed to launch a Dock app
steps.pitfall: Double-clicking is unnecessary and may open two windows; if Safari isn't in the Dock, press **⌘ + Space**, type `Safari`, then press **Return**
